mj-text-bKWuFGBO.content:
Hi {{contact.first_name}},

January marks the moment when conventional wisdom gets expensive.

While most sellers are "waiting for spring" and investors are "staying cautious," the actual data shows something completely different: Q4 2025 multifamily transactions surged 51% year-over-year, and early 2026 single-family activity is showing buyer demand we haven't seen in three years.

Here's what the market isn't telling you: The "wait for spring" strategy is a myth that costs people money. January buyers aren't browsing—they're serious.

And the multifamily sector? It just proved it can absorb historic supply levels and still build momentum heading into 2026.

Translation: The opportunity isn't coming later in 2026. It's here in January. And it disappears the moment everyone else figures it out.

This month, we're breaking down exactly how to capitalize:

✅ How multifamily survived historic supply and built momentum through 2025
✅ Why more buyers are planning to move in 2026—and how to get ready now
✅ January 2026 interest rate snapshot: What you need to know right now

Let's position you ahead of the wave.

mj-text-bKWuFGBO-piT1bAyZ-XkxBz27A.content: → Practical Strategies for Preparing Your Home or Finding Your Next One as Buyer Demand Rises

mj-text-zUU7TNhr.content:
Momentum is building.

New NerdWallet data shows 17% of Americans plan to buy a home in the next 12 months—up from 15% last year. In a market that's been cooling for years, that 2% jump signals a shift.

What this means: Early movers win. Late movers compete.

Planning to move in early 2026? Do these 4 things:

1. Get pre-approved – Understand your buying power at today's rates. Just know: pre-approvals expire in 30-90 days, so time this right.

2. Run the numbers – Calculate your actual budget. Factor in all expenses and your future mortgage payment. Don't stretch too far.

3. Define your non-negotiables – Location, commute, layout, school district, lifestyle needs. Get clear now, decide faster later.

4. Choose your agent early – Find one you trust who understands pricing, competition, timing, and strategy—not just someone who shows homes.

Buying later in 2026? Prepare now.

  • Work on your credit – Pay down debt, make payments on time. Your score impacts your loan terms and rate.

  • Automate your savings – Set up automatic transfers. Remove the temptation to spend it elsewhere.

  • Lean into side hustles – Part-time work, freelance gigs—anything that boosts savings faster.

  • Put windfalls to work – Tax refunds, bonuses, gifts? Straight to your house fund.

The bottom line:

This is the largest year-over-year increase in buyer intent since rates spiked.

The market is waking up. Position yourself now, or compete with everyone else in spring.

mj-text-iwAeSLUN.content: 🏠 How Multifamily Survived and Built Momentum in 2025

mj-text-TwppHz2a.content: → Key Trends, Deals, and Strategies That Kept Multifamily Resilient Through a High-Rate Environment

mj-text-C6AJmHxe.content:
2025 threw everything at multifamily. The sector absorbed 506,000+ new apartments—the most since the 1980s—yet emerged resilient.

🌊 Supply met demand

Over half the new units landed in the South, Texas cities led construction. Despite historic supply, the rental market stayed competitive: Rental Competitiveness Index hit 75.2, up from 74.4 in 2024.

Miami topped the nation with 19 renters competing per vacant apartment—double the national average.

Translation: Supply came. Demand absorbed it. No collapse.

📈 Rent growth recovered

National rents hit $1,755 in March, up 1.0% year-over-year after early-year declines.

New York: +5.5%. Chicago and Kansas City: +3.7%. Columbus: +3.5%.

Renewal rates climbed to 63% nationwide—renters stayed put, keeping competition tight.

🗺️ Geographic split widened

Sun Belt metros (Austin, Phoenix, Tampa) felt pressure from peak deliveries.

Supply-constrained markets saw pricing power return. Washington, D.C. added 23,000 downtown apartmentssince 2020 and topped livability rankings.

💰 Capital surged back

Office-to-apartment conversions broke records: 70,700+ units created from former offices in 2025. D.C. led with 6,533 units in the pipeline.

Transaction volume rebounded—Q4 2025 saw deal volume surge 51% year-over-year as buyers and sellers agreed on valuations.

The bottom line:

Multifamily enters 2026 with momentum.

For investors:

  • Cap rates: 5.5-6.5% (real returns)

  • Conservative underwriting (protects downside)

  • Weak operators exited (less competition)

  • Institutional capital returned (deal flow is back)

The market absorbed massive supply and still performed. That's resilience.

mj-text-DKUspLNS.content: 💰January 2026 Interest Rate Snapshot

mj-text-dcG0v2OP.content:
Here’s where financing stands this month for both multifamily and single-family deals.

Sources: Residential rates sourced from Freddie Mac, LendingTree, Fortune, Yahoo Finance as of January 8-14, 2026. Commercial rates sourced from Commercial Loan Direct, Apartment Loan Store, Select Commercial as of January 11-14, 2026.

mj-text-Lvtk2ipD.content: 🔑 Key Takeaway: Market Wisdom for January 2026

mj-text-sr3BwGDZ.content:
If you're buying or selling single-family:

2026 buyer demand is building now. Early positioning wins.

Sellers: List Q1.

Buyers: Get pre-approved and start looking before spring competition hits.

If you're investing in multifamily:

2025 proved multifamily can absorb historic supply and still build momentum. 2026 is positioned for continued strength.

Deal flow is back. Pricing is clear. Returns are real again—based on operations, not appreciation fantasies.

The question isn't "should I invest?" It's "who am I investing with?"

Because good operators thrived in 2025 while bad operators blamed the market.

mj-text-eY3Yqusd.content: Ready to Stop Waiting and Start Building?

mj-text-3rQjpfCg.content:
We're reviewing portfolios and vetting opportunities for 2026 deployment right now.

Not in March. Not "when things settle down." Now.

Because the investors who build wealth don't time markets perfectly. They position before everyone else realizes the opportunity existed.

Whether you're deploying $50K in your first syndication or managing a $100M family office portfolio, our mission remains the same:

✅ Identify distressed opportunities before they hit the market
✅ Structure favorable acquisitions with optimal debt placement
✅ Execute value-add strategies that create forced appreciation
✅ Time exits for maximum returns when the cycle turns

The opportunities are here. The capital is available. The question is: Will you be positioned to act?

Brian Norton
Principal, Olive Tree Investments
📧 brian@olivetreeinv.io | 📞 404-643-2356
📍 269 Market Place Blvd Suite 126, Cartersville, GA 30121
